justification.personality: The film captures his eccentric nature and love for the unusual, but the book portrays him as more consistently eccentric and less overtly dramatic in his pronouncements.
justification.narrative_role: His role as the eccentric, knowledgeable source of information (especially regarding the Hallows) is maintained, though the film gives him more direct plot involvement.
justification.motivations: His core motivation—sharing unusual knowledge and supporting the unconventional—is present, but the film emphasizes his connection to the Dark Arts history more heavily than the books.
justification.character_arc: The book shows him as a background fixture whose eccentricities are part of the tapestry; the film gives him a more active, albeit brief, role in the main plot revelations, slightly altering his perceived arc.

comparative.key_observations: The film adaptation gives Xenophilius a more pronounced and dramatic role in revealing the Deathly Hallows symbol, whereas the books keep him more firmly in the realm of background eccentricity.
